# Test Fixture Attribution

## zwtv_frontend_fixtures.jl

Front-end-level fixtures for ISO 532-1:2017 clause 6 ("method for
time-varying sounds", colloquially "Method 2" -- not to be confused with
the unrelated ISO 532-2 Moore-Glasberg standard). Generated by
`scripts/generate_mosqito_zwtv_frontend_crosscheck.jl` +
`scripts/crosscheck_zwtv_frontend.py` from MoSQITo `loudness_zwtv` @
commit `d990c33f94f1` (<https://github.com/Eomys/MoSQITo>, Apache
License 2.0), calling its private front-end stage `_third_octave_levels`
(the function this package's `loudness_zwtv` transcribes) and the public
`loudness_zwtv` API (for end-to-end crosscheck), on signals synthesized
by this package's own generator (pure/AM tones).

- `ZWTV_FRONTEND_FIXTURE_CASES`: per case, `band_levels` (28 x nblocks @
  2000 Hz, MoSQITo's `_third_octave_levels` output -- the literal target
  this package's transcription must reproduce), `band_time_axis` (0.5 ms,
  un-decimated), end-to-end `N_t`/`time_axis` (2 ms, from the public
  `loudness_zwtv` API) and `N5_iso`/`N10_iso`. Signals/parameters are the
  SAME as ZwickerLoudness.jl's (the kernel repo) `zwtv_kernel_fixtures.jl`
  cases (`steady_tone_1k_60db`, `tone_burst_1k_70db_30ms`,
  `am_tone_1k_10hz_65db`) -- confirmed by matching `band_levels_sha256`
  hashes across the two independently-generated fixture files -- so this
  package's `band_levels` output is directly comparable to that repo's
  already-vendored kernel fixture, without a runtime dependency on the
  sibling repo. Not derived from any ISO-copyrighted material.
- `ZWTV_ANNEXB_FRONTEND_DERIVED`: derived numbers ONLY (final loudness
  curve, coarsened 25x, plus exact N5/N10) for ISO 532-1:2017 Annex B.4
  "Test signal 6 (tone 250 Hz 30 dB - 80 dB)", computed end-to-end
  (`loudness_zwtv(signal, fs)`, this package feeding
  `ZwickerLoudness.zwicker_loudness_time_varying`) via MoSQITo's
  redistributed copy of that file
  (`validations/sq_metrics/loudness_zwtv/input/ISO_532-1/Annex
  B.4/Test signal 6 (tone 250 Hz 30 dB - 80 dB).wav`). The underlying
  `.wav` is ISO-copyrighted material; MoSQITo's Apache-2.0 grant does not
  extend to it, so it is never read by or shipped with this repository --
  see ZwickerLoudness.jl's `.superpowers/sdd/zwtv-pins.md` for the full
  licensing reasoning, which this repo's fixture generation mirrors
  exactly (same signal, same calibration factor, same coarsening). Only
  MoSQITo's *computed results* on that signal are vendored here. Byte-
  identical to the kernel repo's `ZWTV_ANNEXB_DERIVED.N_t_coarse`/
  `time_axis_coarse`, confirming the two repos' Annex B pipelines agree
  end-to-end.

## `band_levels`/`_third_octave_levels` transcription (`src/zwtv_frontend.jl`)

The one-third-octave filter coefficient tables (`THIRD_OCTAVE_FILTER_REF`,
`THIRD_OCTAVE_FILTER_DIFF`, `FILTER_GAIN`) and the squaring/smoothing
stage in `src/zwtv_frontend.jl` are a faithful transcription of MoSQITo's
`_third_octave_levels.py` and `_square_and_smooth.py` @ commit
`d990c33f94f1` (<https://github.com/Eomys/MoSQITo>, Apache License 2.0),
which themselves cite ISO 532-1:2017 Annex A (filter design) and section
6.3 (calculation algorithm). Per-function citations are in the source
file itself.
